subject: [PATCH v5 33/36] Documentation/mm: describe private (N_MEMORY_PRIVATE) memory nodes

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation for private memory nodes, which are NUMA nodes whose memory is hotplugged by a driver and deliberately hidden from the kernel's normal memory management. The documentation covers the isolation model, ZONELIST_PRIVATE, driver provisioning API, capability opt-in model, and observability surfaces. Private memory nodes are marked as N_MEMORY_PRIVATE instead of N_MEMORY and are not considered by the page allocator's normal or fallback paths. This allows drivers to provide a block of NUMA-addressable memory that the rest of the kernel will not allocate from on its own.
